ops.title: supervise: fail-closed nominate/corroborate/act contract
ops.body:
# The safety contract

A staleness signal only NOMINATES death; an authoritative Alive probe must corroborate before any action, and every probe error reads as not-confirmed-dead — an ambiguous signal never kills or respawns a possibly-live child. Kill and respawn run under the same per-subject lock as the consumer's manual-kill path (Subject.Lock must return the SAME locker for a subject's identity across every Subject value — back it with KeyedMutex), and the subject's status is re-read under that lock immediately before acting, so a child killed or respawned by another path between nomination and action is detected, not double-actioned.

# Loop mechanics

- Roster enumeration failure is "no signal": the tick aborts and retries next interval, never "all subjects gone".
- StartupGrace suppresses nomination for a freshly (re)spawned subject, so it is never mistaken for dead.
- Stale errors fold to false ("no signal"); Alive errors mean do nothing this tick.
- Kill runs on an already-confirmed-dead process (husk teardown) and must be safe there; Respawn starts fresh; Park records the terminal state once the restart budget is exhausted, after which Active must return false so the parked subject is never re-actioned.
- respawnUnderBudget records the strike BEFORE the respawn, so a respawn that itself fails still counts toward the budget and a persistently-dying subject climbs to a park.
- pruneStrikes drops history for names the roster no longer surfaces; a name that leaves and returns starts with a fresh budget.
- A subject hook failure aborts the tick; survivors retry next interval. OnError observes tick errors, never stops the loop.
